assessments.status_rationale: Today’s headlines provided do not indicate Kraken has publicly filed an IPO registration statement (S-1/F-1) on SEC EDGAR. Deadline is 2026-12-31.

A MarketWatch item discusses the potential implications of an Amazon-Globalstar purchase, but there is no clear evidence in the provided news that the acquisition has completed/closed (e.g., an announced deal close or a Globalstar 8-K stating the merger has closed).
No provided item shows Meta publicly stating that in-house/custom accelerators handle at least 25% of its AI inference compute in production. Deadline is 2026-10-31.
No NY Fed Household Debt and Credit Report (2026 Q3 release) data is referenced in today’s bullets; no evidence of 90+ day auto-loan delinquency share at or above 4.5% is provided.

Despite extensive Iran-war coverage, the provided news does not show a publicly traded crude-oil tanker owner/operator announcing a 7+ day suspension/avoidance of Strait of Hormuz transits due to security risk.

No evidence in today’s bullets indicates OpenAI has reduced listed pricing (USD per 1M input tokens) for its flagship general-purpose API model by ≥20% versus 2026-04-15 published pricing.

Today’s items include a CNBC piece on cattle prices but do not reference CDC reporting of a confirmed U.S. human H5N1 case with an exposure narrative explicitly involving dairy cattle contact or raw milk handling/consumption.

No provided news indicates the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation has published a draft public post-2026 Colorado River operations document including quantified accounting changes for evaporation/system losses.
